title: populus × glandulosa
intro: Populus × glandulosa, also known as Cottonwood or Black Cottonwood, is a hybrid deciduous tree species that is native to North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Populus × glandulosa include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
